answer:
The verse you are referring to is the second couplet (sher) of the ghazal, where Ghalib describes building a wall-less house with **no neighbor (humsaya)** and **no guard/door-keeper (pasban)**—terms that evoke solitude free from companionship or intrusion.[^1]

**Translation:**  
A house I'll build of neither wall nor stone,  
No neighbor there, no guard, and be alone.[^1]

**Transliteration:**  
Be-dar-o-deewar sa ek ghar banaya chahiye  
Koi humsaya na ho aur pasban koi na ho[^2]

**Original (Urdu):**  
بے_در_و_دیوار سا اک گھر بنایا چاہیے  
کوئی ہمسایہ نہ ہو اور پاسباں کوئی نہ ہو[^3]

[^1]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [paragraph](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/168-rahiye-ab-aisi-jagah-chal-kar-jahan-koi-na-ho/translation/#p-09bdfe).
[^2]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [chapter](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/168-rahiye-ab-aisi-jagah-chal-kar-jahan-koi-na-ho/transliteration/).
[^3]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [chapter](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/168-rahiye-ab-aisi-jagah-chal-kar-jahan-koi-na-ho/original/).

args.query: "no neighbour" OR "no companion" OR "no door-keeper" ghalib
